commands.review_summary:
The PR changes reply-session initialization revision hashing to use the canonical persisted session-entry shape, strips runtime-only resolved skills before hashing, and adds a focused regression test.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +56. Total +88 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main JSON-stringifies the whole SessionEntry for the guarded revision while storage can represent the same skills snapshot as persisted promptRef, hydrated prompt, or runtime-only resolvedSkills. I did not run the regression test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs contributor-side real behavior proof and final CI, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(sessions): compare reply init revisions using persisted shape
target.body:
## Summary
- Compare reply-session initialization revisions against the canonical persisted session entry shape.
- Strip runtime-only `skillsSnapshot.resolvedSkills` before revision hashing.
- Normalize hydrated skill prompts back to persisted `promptRef` shape so disk snapshots and live writer cache compare consistently.
- Add a regression test for a runtime-only resolved-skills cache during reply session initialization.

## Why
Telegram `/new` and `/reset` can fail in topic sessions even when no real concurrent session change occurred. The snapshot side reads persisted state from disk, while the commit side may inspect the live in-memory writer cache with runtime-only skill cache data and hydrated prompt content. The old raw `JSON.stringify(entry)` revision check treated those equivalent states as different, returning `stale-snapshot` and surfacing `reply session initialization conflicted`.

In production this showed up as Telegram spooled update retries and `/new` checkmarks that did not actually rotate the topic session.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts -t "runtime-only resolvedSkills"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.skills-stripping.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/config/sessions/store-writer.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/store-load.ts src/config/sessions/store.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## Runtime evidence from local deployment
After applying the fix and restarting the gateway:
- health endpoint returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`
- gateway connectivity probe was `ok`
- running task count was `0`
- Telegram ingress spool files were `0`
- post-fix log scan found `0` `reply session initialization conflicted` entries and `0` failed spooled updates
